framework.name: NCSC Incident Management Guidance
framework.scope: Guidance on preparing for, detecting, responding to, and recovering from cyber security incidents, aimed at medium to large organisations
framework.structure_description: Controls covering incident response planning, detection, triage, containment, eradication, recovery, and post-incident review

controls.title: Develop an incident response plan
controls.description: Create and maintain a documented incident response plan that defines roles, responsibilities, communication channels, escalation procedures, and decision-making authority for responding to cyber security incidents.
controls.description_nl: Create and maintain a documented incident response plan that defines roles, responsibilities, communication channels, escalation procedures, and decision-making authority for responding to cyber security incidents.
controls.implementation_guidance: Document an incident response plan covering all phases. Define RACI matrix for incident roles. Include contact lists for internal and external stakeholders. Store plans where they are accessible during an outage.
controls.verification_guidance: Review incident response plan. Verify it is current and accessible. Check RACI matrix completeness.

controls.title: Establish an incident response team
controls.description: Designate an incident response team with defined roles: incident manager, technical lead, communications lead, and legal/regulatory liaison. Team members should be trained and available outside business hours for critical incidents.
controls.description_nl: Designate an incident response team with defined roles: incident manager, technical lead, communications lead, and legal/regulatory liaison. Team members should be trained and available outside business hours for critical incidents.
controls.implementation_guidance: Assign incident response roles. Provide role-specific training. Establish on-call rotas. Ensure backup personnel for each role.
controls.verification_guidance: Verify team roster and role assignments. Check training completion. Test on-call availability.

controls.title: Establish reporting channels
controls.description: Provide clear channels for staff to report suspected security incidents. Reports should reach the incident response team quickly. Encourage reporting by making the process simple and ensuring reporters are not penalised.
controls.description_nl: Provide clear channels for staff to report suspected security incidents. Reports should reach the incident response team quickly. Encourage reporting by making the process simple and ensuring reporters are not penalised.
controls.implementation_guidance: Establish dedicated incident reporting channels (email, phone, ticketing system). Communicate these to all staff. Train staff on what to report. Implement a no-blame reporting culture.
controls.verification_guidance: Test reporting channels. Verify staff awareness of how to report. Review reporting volumes.

controls.title: Triage and classify incidents
controls.description: Implement a triage process to quickly assess the severity and scope of reported incidents. Classify incidents using a consistent severity scale (e.g., P1-P4) that drives response timelines and escalation requirements.
controls.description_nl: Implement a triage process to quickly assess the severity and scope of reported incidents. Classify incidents using a consistent severity scale (e.g., P1-P4) that drives response timelines and escalation requirements.
controls.implementation_guidance: Define severity classification criteria (scope, impact, data sensitivity). Establish triage procedures. Set response time targets per severity level. Train triage staff.
controls.verification_guidance: Review classification criteria. Verify triage procedures are followed. Check response times against targets.

controls.title: Contain the incident
controls.description: Take immediate containment actions to prevent the incident from spreading. Short-term containment stops the immediate damage; long-term containment allows continued operation while a full fix is developed.
controls.description_nl: Take immediate containment actions to prevent the incident from spreading. Short-term containment stops the immediate damage; long-term containment allows continued operation while a full fix is developed.
controls.implementation_guidance: Define containment strategies per incident type (network isolation, account disablement, system shutdown). Balance containment speed against business impact and evidence preservation. Document all containment actions.
controls.verification_guidance: Review containment playbooks. Verify containment actions are documented. Test containment capabilities.

controls.title: Eradicate the threat
controls.description: After containment, identify and remove the root cause of the incident. This may involve removing malware, closing vulnerabilities, resetting compromised credentials, and rebuilding affected systems from known-good sources.
controls.description_nl: After containment, identify and remove the root cause of the incident. This may involve removing malware, closing vulnerabilities, resetting compromised credentials, and rebuilding affected systems from known-good sources.
controls.implementation_guidance: Identify all affected systems. Remove malware and attacker persistence mechanisms. Patch exploited vulnerabilities. Reset compromised credentials. Rebuild systems where integrity is uncertain.
controls.verification_guidance: Verify eradication completeness. Scan for remaining compromise indicators. Confirm vulnerability patches are applied.

controls.title: Recover services
controls.description: Restore affected services to normal operation. Recovery should be phased, with enhanced monitoring during the recovery period to detect any recurrence of the incident.
controls.description_nl: Restore affected services to normal operation. Recovery should be phased, with enhanced monitoring during the recovery period to detect any recurrence of the incident.
controls.implementation_guidance: Restore from known-good backups. Apply hardening before returning systems to service. Implement enhanced monitoring during recovery. Validate system integrity before full restoration.
controls.verification_guidance: Verify restoration from trusted sources. Check enhanced monitoring is in place. Validate system integrity.

controls.title: Manage communications during incidents
controls.description: Coordinate internal and external communications during incidents. Include notification to regulators (ICO, sector regulators), law enforcement where appropriate, and affected individuals where personal data is involved.
controls.description_nl: Coordinate internal and external communications during incidents. Include notification to regulators (ICO, sector regulators), law enforcement where appropriate, and affected individuals where personal data is involved.
controls.implementation_guidance: Pre-draft communication templates for common incident types. Define regulatory notification requirements and timelines (ICO 72-hour notification for personal data breaches). Establish media handling procedures.
controls.verification_guidance: Review communication templates. Verify regulatory notification procedures. Check media handling plan.

controls.title: Conduct post-incident review
controls.description: After each significant incident, conduct a formal post-incident review (lessons learned). Identify what worked well, what did not, and what changes should be made to prevent recurrence and improve response.
controls.description_nl: After each significant incident, conduct a formal post-incident review (lessons learned). Identify what worked well, what did not, and what changes should be made to prevent recurrence and improve response.
controls.implementation_guidance: Conduct post-incident reviews within 2 weeks of incident closure. Include all involved parties. Document findings, root cause, and improvement actions. Track improvement action completion.
controls.verification_guidance: Review post-incident reports. Verify improvement actions are tracked and completed.

controls.title: Exercise the incident response plan
controls.description: Regularly test the incident response plan through tabletop exercises and simulations. Exercises should cover a range of scenarios including ransomware, data breach, supply chain compromise, and denial of service.
controls.description_nl: Regularly test the incident response plan through tabletop exercises and simulations. Exercises should cover a range of scenarios including ransomware, data breach, supply chain compromise, and denial of service.
controls.implementation_guidance: Conduct tabletop exercises at least annually. Include technical and non-technical participants. Vary scenarios to cover different incident types. Update plans based on exercise findings.
controls.verification_guidance: Verify exercise schedule and completion. Review exercise reports. Check that findings are incorporated into plans.
